specimens.hold_reason: K +6.3 SD above baseline; Ca −4.8 SD below baseline
specimens.hold_detail: K measured at 6.9 mmol/L against patient P002 baseline of 5.0 ± 0.30 mmol/L (+6.3 SD). Ca measured at 7.1 mg/dL against baseline of 9.0 ± 0.40 mg/dL (−4.8 SD). Concurrent acute K elevation with Ca depression is the hallmark EDTA anticoagulant contamination signature — EDTA chelates calcium, causing artefactual hypocalcaemia while potassium leaches from cells. Both analytes exceed the CLSI EP33 delta-check threshold independently; their co-occurrence makes contamination near-certain.

specimens.hold_reason: Glucose +10.8 SD above P001 baseline; profile fits P004
specimens.hold_detail: Glucose measured at 221 mg/dL against patient P001 baseline of 92 ± 12 mg/dL (+10.8 SD) — an extreme outlier for this patient. Pairwise swap analysis found that reassigning S105 to patient P004 (glucose baseline 218 ± 25 mg/dL) reduces the total weighted mismatch by 97%. S106, assigned to P004, shows the mirror pattern with glucose 90 mg/dL fitting P001’s baseline instead. The agent correctly identified both specimens as a swapped pair rather than treating the glucose anomaly as a single-specimen error.

specimens.hold_reason: Glucose −5.1 SD below P004 baseline; profile fits P001
specimens.hold_detail: Glucose measured at 90 mg/dL against patient P004 baseline of 218 ± 25 mg/dL (−5.1 SD). Pairwise swap analysis found that reassigning S106 to patient P001 (glucose baseline 92 ± 12 mg/dL) reduces the total weighted mismatch by 97%. This specimen is the swap partner of S105 — the two were collected from the correct patients but labelled in reverse. Both are held pending re-labelling; neither analyte profile indicates contamination.

specimens.hold_reason: K +4.0 SD above baseline; Ca −3.0 SD below baseline
specimens.hold_detail: K measured at 6.2 mmol/L against patient P002 baseline of 5.0 ± 0.30 mmol/L (+4.0 SD). Ca measured at 7.8 mg/dL against baseline of 9.0 ± 0.40 mg/dL (−3.0 SD). Both analytes breach the CLSI EP33 delta-check threshold simultaneously. The co-occurrence pattern is consistent with EDTA tube contamination. Note: S107 presents a subtler signal than S101 (contamination score 1.41 vs 1.50) — likely a smaller volume of EDTA carry-over — but the delta-check correctly flags it.

specimens.truth_category: CKD interference guard — chronic hyperkalemia, Ca preserved
